message:
feat(dashboard): add shimmer animation for high context warnings

Enhance visual feedback for high context usage (>80%) with shimmer
animations that sync with animTick:

- RenderContextMiniBar: Warning indicators (! and !!) now shimmer
  with yellow/peach (80-90%) and red/maroon (90%+) gradients
- renderContextBar: Warning icons shimmer when context exceeds 80%
- renderDetailContextBar: Filled portion shimmers red when >80%
  (now accepts tick parameter for animation sync)
- RenderPaneDetail: Updated signature to pass tick for shimmer

This creates an eye-catching visual effect that draws attention to
agents approaching context limits, helping users notice and respond
to potential compaction issues.

Closes: ntm-52gk

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-k7o","title":"Integrate --ssh flag with all session commands","description":"Add --ssh support to spawn, list, attach, send, status, kill.

## What This Task Does

After the SSH wrapper (ntm-5zs) is complete, integrate --ssh flag
with all commands that operate on sessions.

## Commands to Update

- ntm spawn --ssh host: Create session on remote
- ntm list --ssh host: List remote sessions
- ntm attach --ssh host: Attach to remote session
- ntm send --ssh host: Send prompt to remote agents
- ntm status --ssh host: Get remote session status
- ntm kill --ssh host: Kill remote session

## Implementation Pattern

For each command:
1. Add --ssh flag (string, optional)
2. Pass to TmuxClient constructor
3. All tmux operations use client abstraction

func runSpawn(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    sshHost, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("ssh")
    client := tmux.NewClient(sshHost)
    
    // All operations use client
    if err := client.CreateSession(session); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    ...
}

## Session Host Persistence

When spawning remotely, store host in tmux environment:
client.SetEnvironment(session, "NTM_REMOTE_HOST", sshHost)

When operating on existing session without --ssh:
1. Get session's NTM_REMOTE_HOST
2. If set, use it
3. If not, assume local

## Error Handling

- Connection timeout: Clear message, suggest checking connectivity
- Auth failure: Suggest ssh-agent or key path
- Remote tmux not found: Suggest installing tmux on remote

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --ssh works on spawn
- [ ] --ssh works on list  
- [ ] --ssh works on attach
- [ ] --ssh works on send
- [ ] --ssh works on status
- [ ] --ssh works on kill
- [ ] Remote host auto-detected for existing sessions

 {"id":"ntm-l4fp","title":"Add Agent Mail notifications for scan results","description":"## Overview
Integrate UBS scan results with Agent Mail to notify coordinating agents about code quality findings, enabling proactive collaboration on fixes.

## Background
When multiple AI agents work on a codebase, scan results are valuable coordination signals:
- New critical findings need immediate attention
- Findings in files an agent is editing should be surfaced
- Scan summaries help agents prioritize work

## Implementation Details

### Notification Types
1. **Scan Summary** - After each scan, send digest to all active agents
   - Total findings by severity
   - New vs. existing findings
   - Files with most issues

2. **Targeted Alerts** - Notify specific agents about relevant findings
   - Match findings to file reservations
   - Agent editing auth.go gets auth-related findings
   - Importance: high for critical, normal for others

3. **Fix Confirmations** - When findings are resolved
   - Notify original reporter
   - Update thread if findings were discussed

### Message Templates
```markdown
Subject: [Scan] {n} new findings in {project}

## Scan Summary
- Critical: {critical_count}
- Errors: {error_count}  
- Warnings: {warning_count}

## New Findings
{finding_list}

## Your Files
The following findings affect files you're working on:
{agent_relevant_findings}
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/scanner/notify.go - Notification logic
- internal/scanner/matcher.go - File reservation matching
- internal/cli/scan.go - Add --notify flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scan summaries sent to registered agents
- [ ] Findings matched to file reservations
- [ ] Appropriate importance levels set
- [ ] Notifications are concise and actionable","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:33:31.76544-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T16:56:58.89813-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T16:56:58.89813-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-l4fp","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:58.079729-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l4fp","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:13.789853-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-l5h","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure","description":"# EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure

## Background & Rationale

NTM (Named Tmux Manager) currently has very low test coverage across most packages. This EPIC addresses the need for:

1. **Full Unit Test Coverage** - Testing each function/method in isolation WITHOUT mocks or fakes where possible, using real dependencies
2. **Integration Tests** - Testing component interactions (CLI → tmux, config → commands, etc.)
3. **End-to-End (E2E) Tests** - Full workflow tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Current Coverage Analysis

| Package | Current Coverage | Status |
|---------|-----------------|--------|
| cmd/ntm | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/cli | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/config | 16.2% | 🟡 Needs improvement |
| internal/output | 55.1% | 🟡 Good start |
| internal/palette | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/robot | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/status | 69.6% | 🟢 Good (has failures) |
| internal/tmux | 5.4% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tui/* | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tutorial | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/updater | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/watcher | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |

## Testing Philosophy

### No Mocks/Fakes Policy
- Use REAL tmux sessions for tmux tests (create/destroy per test)
- Use REAL file system for config tests
- Use REAL HTTP calls where applicable
- Only mock external services that are impractical (GitHub API rate limits, etc.)

### Detailed Logging Strategy
- All E2E tests output structured logs with timestamps
- Log file per test run with full command output
- Capture stderr/stdout separately
- Include system state before/after each test

## Success Criteria
- 80%+ coverage on all non-TUI packages
- 60%+ coverage on TUI packages (harder to test without mocks)
- All E2E tests pass on macOS and Linux
- Tests complete in <5 minutes total
- No flaky tests (3 consecutive passes required)

## Technical Considerations
- Tests requiring tmux must be skipped in CI without tmux
- Use Go's testing.Short() for quick vs full test modes
- Parallelize where safe (different tmux sessions)
- Clean up resources even on test failure","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:17:38.345103-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:48:43.676311-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-uwa","title":"Implement Agent Mail server detection and config","description":"# Implement Agent Mail Server Detection and Config

## Purpose

Before using any Agent Mail features, NTM needs to detect if the server is running and load configuration.

## Configuration Schema

Add to ~/.config/ntm/config.toml:
```toml
[agent_mail]
enabled = true                              # Master toggle
url = "http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/"          # Server endpoint
token = ""                                  # Bearer token (or use env var)
auto_register = true                        # Auto-register sessions as agents
program_name = "ntm"                        # Program identifier for registration
```

Environment variable overrides:
- AGENT_MAIL_URL
- AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN
- AGENT_MAIL_ENABLED (0/1)

## Detection Logic

```go
func (c *Client) IsAvailable() bool {
    // 1. Check if enabled in config
    // 2. Attempt health_check() with 2s timeout
    // 3. Cache result for 30s to avoid repeated checks
}

func (c *Client) RequireAvailable() error {
    if !c.IsAvailable() {
        return ErrAgentMailUnavailable
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **ntm deps**: Show Agent Mail status
   ```
   Agent Mail: ✓ running (http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/)
   Agent Mail: ✗ not detected (optional)
   ```

2. **Config loading**: internal/config loads agent_mail section
3. **Lazy initialization**: Client created on first use, not at startup
4. **Status command**: Show connection status in ntm status

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config schema added with sensible defaults
- [ ] Environment variables override config
- [ ] Detection is fast (<2s timeout) and cached
- [ ] ntm deps shows Agent Mail status
- [ ] Graceful behavior when server unavailable","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:01.67162-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:32:32.28481-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:32:32.28481-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-uwa","depends_on_id":"ntm-ceb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.651713-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 	t := m.theme

@@ -1434,11 +1422,26 @@ func (m Model) renderContextBar(percent float64, width int) string {
 	barContent := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100.0, barWidth, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)
 
 	percentStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Overlay)
-	warningStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Red).Bold(true)
+
+	// Determine warning icon with shimmer effect for high context
+	var warningIcon string
+	switch {
+	case percent >= 95:
+		// Critical: shimmer the warning in red/maroon gradient
+		warningIcon = " " + styles.Shimmer("!!!", m.animTick, string(t.Red), string(t.Maroon), string(t.Red))
+	case percent >= 90:
+		// High: shimmer in red/orange gradient
+		warningIcon = " " + styles.Shimmer("!!", m.animTick, string(t.Red), string(t.Maroon), string(t.Red))
+	case percent >= 80:
+		// Warning: shimmer in yellow/orange gradient
+		warningIcon = " " + styles.Shimmer("!", m.animTick, string(t.Yellow), string(t.Peach), string(t.Yellow))
+	default:
+		warningIcon = ""
+	}
 
 	bar := "[" + barContent + "]" +
 		percentStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%3.0f%%", percent)) +
-		warningStyle.Render(warningIcon)
+		warningIcon
 
 	return bar
 }
